name: Antech Diagnostics Veterinary Laboratory API
what_it_does: Antech Diagnostics (Mars Petcare / VCA subsidiary) veterinary laboratory REST API for diagnostic test ordering, result delivery, and clinical data integration for veterinary practices and animal hospitals. Enables AI agents to manage laboratory test order submission and result retrieval for veterinary diagnostic workflow automation, handle result notification and critical value alerting for patient safety automation, access CBC, chemistry, endocrinology, and pathology result normalization for veterinary clinical data integration, retrieve species-specific reference range application for result interpretation automation, manage preventive care panel and wellness testing for proactive health monitoring automation, handle STAT and urgent test priority for time-sensitive diagnostic automation, access digital pathology and cytology image retrieval for diagnostic imaging integration, retrieve test catalog and order code lookup for diagnostic ordering automation, manage account and pricing data for veterinary practice financial automation, and integrate Antech with VCA, Banfield, veterinary PMS platforms (ezyVet, Cornerstone) for end-to-end veterinary diagnostic ecosystem.
use_cases: Ordering vet lab tests from clinical workflow automation agents
Retrieving diagnostic results from patient record agents
Alerting critical values from patient safety agents
Managing wellness panels from preventive care agents
best_when: A veterinary practice, animal hospital, or veterinary software platform wanting AI agents to automate laboratory test ordering, result integration, critical value alerting, and preventive care panel management within Antech's veterinary reference laboratory network.
avoid_when: CRITICAL VALUE PATIENT SAFETY NOTIFICATION: Automated Antech result processing must implement critical value alerting for life-threatening findings (severe anemia, critical electrolyte abnormality, suspected toxicosis); automated result filing without critical value notification creates patient safety gap for missed urgent findings requiring immediate clinical response. VCPR REQUIREMENT FOR DIAGNOSTIC ORDERS: Automated diagnostic test ordering via Antech requires valid VCPR for the ordering veterinarian and patient; automated bulk test ordering without confirmed VCPR creates veterinary practice act compliance issue in most states. VCA/BANFIELD NETWORK INTEGRATION EXCLUSIVITY: Antech is Mars Petcare subsidiary with strategic integration with VCA and Banfield pet hospitals; automated integration of Antech results for non-Mars-network practices may have different API access terms; verify access tier and pricing for independent practice vs Mars-affiliated hospital integration.

auth.notes: Antech uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON and HL7. Los Angeles, California HQ. Founded 1987 by Charles Vitt. Mars Petcare subsidiary (via VCA acquisition 2017, $9.1B). 100+ reference laboratories globally. 30,000+ veterinary practice clients. Full diagnostic menu: hematology, chemistry, endocrinology, cytology, histopathology, infectious disease. Digital pathology. Mobile app for results. Competes with IDEXX Laboratories for veterinary reference laboratory market.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-test pricing with practice account
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-test pricing by test category; VCA/Banfield network pricing; independent practice negotiated rates; API access with active Antech practice account
pricing.notes: Los Angeles CA (Mars Petcare). Per-test laboratory pricing. VCA/Banfield network integrated pricing. Independent practice negotiated rates. API with active practice account.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; result and order queries throttled; webhooks for result ready events

known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL VALUE NOTIFICATION MANDATORY: Antech result processing must implement species-specific critical value thresholds and route to veterinarian for immediate notification; automated result filing without critical value check creates patient safety risk; implement critical value alerting for hematologic, chemistry, and infectious disease critical thresholds

HL7 FORMAT FOR PRACTICE MANAGEMENT INTEGRATION: Antech result delivery uses HL7 ORU messages for PMS integration (Cornerstone, ezyVet, AVImark); automated result routing to non-HL7-capable PMS requires HL7 parsing; verify target PMS HL7 support or use Antech REST API result retrieval as alternative

MARS/VCA NETWORK INTEGRATION TIERING: Antech has differentiated API access for VCA/Banfield network hospitals vs independent practices; automated integration must use appropriate API tier credentials for practice type; VCA hospital credentials may not function for independent practice integration

RESULT AMENDMENT CHANGE DETECTION: Antech may amend results after initial release (QC review, cytology review, confirmatory testing); automated result processing must handle amended result notifications and update previously filed records; automated processing without amendment detection creates outdated clinical record

SPECIES REFERENCE RANGE FOR EXOTIC ANIMALS: Antech species coverage includes exotic species (ferrets, rabbits, birds, reptiles) with distinct reference ranges; automated reference range application must include species identifier; exotic species results compared to canine/feline reference ranges create false clinical alerts

ORDER PRIORITY STAT VS ROUTINE TURNAROUND: Antech STAT (same-day) vs routine (24-48 hour) test order creates different result turnaround and pricing; automated test ordering must correctly specify order priority for clinical urgency; routine order for STAT-urgent tests creates delayed diagnosis
agent_readiness.security_notes: Veterinary diagnostics. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Animal patient records and veterinary laboratory data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Veterinary laboratory. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Animal patient records and laboratory result data.
